mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate from elevated levels signals monetary policy pivot toward accommodation. This reduction in the cost of capital compresses equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as investors reprice growth assets upward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by more than 0.5 percentage points within 30 calendar days
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.58% as of 2020-02-15 reflects recent emergency cuts
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines >0.5pp, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. This contraction in aggregate demand produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations are revised downward across cyclical sectors.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points in a single month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2020-02-15, historically low but vulnerable to shocks
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp in a month, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is positive (r > 0.1) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales volume (RSAFS) reflects real consumer spending momentum. A sustained decline in monthly retail sales indicates weakening end-demand and inventory destocking, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward earnings guidance deteriorates.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of instances where RSAFS declines >2% MoM, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS changes and forward returns is positive (rho > 0.08) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) measures real economic capacity utilization and manufacturing health. A sharp decline in INDPRO signals supply-side contraction and reduced corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 1.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 62% of instances where INDPRO declines >1.5% MoM, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal consumer and construction sector health. When both decline simultaneously, this indicates broad-based demand destruction across residential and consumption channels, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as forward GDP growth expectations are revised sharply downward.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% MoM AND RSAFS declines >2% MoM in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1567K and RSAFS at 529766M as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across residential and consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline simultaneously by specified thresholds, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate (UNRATE) move inversely during labor market transitions. A sharp divergence—rising UNRATE despite stable PAYEMS—signals hidden labor market weakness and forward-looking job losses, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >0.2pp while PAYEMS growth is <0.1% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 152544K and UNRATE at 3.5% as of 2020-02-15
regime_hint: labor market inflection point or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of instances where UNRATE rises >0.2pp and PAYEMS growth <0.1% MoM, or interaction coefficient is not significant (p > 0.15) in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-rate environment (FEDFUNDS <2%), inflation measured by CPIAUCSL becomes the binding constraint on further monetary easing. Rising inflation signals the Fed cannot cut rates further without currency/inflation risk, limiting equity upside and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as real rates compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.58% and CPIAUCSL at 259.05 as of 2020-02-15
regime_hint: monetary constraint regime with low policy rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS <2% and CPIAUCSL rises >0.3% MoM, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12) in any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) represents the broadest measure of economic activity. In a late-cycle expansion (GDPC1 growth slowing), a sharp decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals demand peak and imminent recession, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings are revised downward across all sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >8% from prior quarter AND GDPC1 growth is <2% annualized
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19220.49B as of 2020-10-15 (latest available), HOUST at 1567K as of 2020-01-15
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand peak signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 58% of instances where HOUST declines >8% and GDPC1 growth <2%, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
